mission: Teach agents to build, benchmark, and honestly compare custom HIP kernels for argmax, top-k, beam-search helpers, logits processors, and token sampling against hipCUB, vLLM on ROCm, vLLM, FlashInfer, Triton, and framework-generated baselines.

must_not_claim: top-p equivalence from a top-k-only kernel
fair serving comparison unless logits processors, RNG, workspace, and launch boundaries are recorded
sorted top-k equivalence when the custom kernel returns unsorted candidates

upstream_references.name: vLLM on ROCm sampling and beam helpers
vLLM sampler, top-k/top-p, beam search, and logits processors
FlashInfer top-k, top-p, and sampling references

benchmark_scaffolds.contract: Top-k candidates sorted by descending logit with lower token id winning ties, followed by temperature softmax over top-k and sampling with precomputed per-row uniforms.
baselines_to_add: hipCUB DeviceTopK plus separate sampling kernel
vLLM on ROCm top-k sampling path
framework torch.topk plus torch.multinomial or equivalent
custom_next_steps: Replace shared-memory list merge with warp-specialized merge for small vocabularies
Add a hipCUB BlockReduce candidate-list variant inside the custom kernel
Fuse simple logits processors before candidate insertion
Add top-p filtering as a separate contract rather than overloading top-k results
Measure sorted versus unsorted top-k when the sampler does not need sorted output

planned_tasks.objective: Fuse mask, bias, repetition penalty, and argmax for greedy decode.
planned_tasks.custom_strategy: Use value-index pair reductions with deterministic tie and mask policy.
negative_examples: Argmax tie policy differs from framework baseline
A separate processor pass plus hipCUB ArgMax remains faster for large batches

planned_tasks.objective: Select beam expansions and parent ids from beam-width by vocab scores.
planned_tasks.custom_strategy: Fuse beam score update, length penalty, EOS handling, and top-k parent materialization.
negative_examples: Full sort of all beam-token candidates loses to library or serving helper
Finished-beam edge cases make the custom result invalid

planned_tasks.objective: Study exact or approximate top-p cutoff, probability renormalization, and sampling.
planned_tasks.custom_strategy: Compare sorted-prefix, histogram/AIR-style, and fixed-vocab specialized paths.
negative_examples: Top-p cutoff includes a different boundary token than the oracle
Histogram approximation is faster but violates the required sampling contract

planned_tasks.objective: Select top-k experts with routing bias, grouping, score normalization, and metadata writes.
planned_tasks.custom_strategy: Fuse expert score transform and top-k with compact routing output.
negative_examples: Register pressure from k=8 sorted outputs erases fusion gains
Routing replay or grouped-policy output differs from serving-stack expectations
